note: Every modification the editor image makes to the upstream build, as typed units. The DEPLOYED build is editor/Dockerfile.online (Collabora Online from source, wired in docker-compose.yml): it merges the translation data from editor/l10n/overrides/client/*.json into the built catalogs and applies the code/asset/theme patches below to the from-source output. editor/Dockerfile (prebuilt-base patching) is the stopgap/fallback and still uses inline sed for PO-L10N-006. dockerfile_lines below refer to the stopgap editor/Dockerfile and are approximate; the same patches live in editor/Dockerfile.online.

patches.title: Fold glass theme into bundle.css + external po-toggle.js theme gate
patches.rationale: Theme is part of the editor's own stylesheet (correct on first paint), gated by a same-origin external script (CSP-safe), not an override sheet.
patches.rollback: Drop the cat>>bundle.css and the two cool.html sed lines; remove the po-toggle.js COPY.

patches.title: Delete dead inline dark-theme <script> from cool.html
patches.rationale: Inline script violates the binary-baked CSP on every load and never runs (gated on ?darkTheme, which our integration never sets); deletion is behavior-preserving.
patches.rollback: Remove the sed; the dead block returns on rebuild.

patches.title: Rewrite Collabora product/copyright display strings to PrivateOffice
patches.rationale: MPL-2.0 permits modifying this self-hosted build; phrases first, then the bare word on data/markup files only (not the bundle.js global).

patches.title: De-concatenate ribbon group expander 'More options for {1}'
patches.rationale: Upstream builds 'More options for '+id as a raw concat (never translated, leaks the control id); route through the existing catalog key 'More options for {1}'.
patches.rollback: Revert the sed to the original concat.

patches.title: De-concatenate combobox accessible name 'Open {name}'
patches.rationale: Upstream composes _('Open ')+value+_(' list') (un-localizable fragments leak English / freeze word order); route through the existing 'Open {name}' key.

patches.title: Client catalog string injections (12 langs)
patches.render_path: client (UNVERIFIED per string: catalog presence does not prove the string renders from the client catalog - some render from core. Verify each, see the Clipboard caveat in assisted_translate.md).
patches.rationale: Fills missing client _() keys the merged catalog omits. The values are now extracted to editor/l10n/overrides/client/<lang>.json as canonical data.
patches.rollback: Remove the overrides merge step in editor/Dockerfile.online (and the 12 sed lines in the stopgap editor/Dockerfile).
patches.status: consumed: editor/Dockerfile.online merges editor/l10n/overrides/client/*.json into the built ui-<lang>.json (migration done, build-verified). The stopgap editor/Dockerfile still injects via inline sed.

patches.title: Neutralize upstream URLs to about:blank
patches.rationale: No phone-home and no upstream-identity leak via header-logo / release-notes / docs / proxy links.
patches.rollback: Remove the two grep|sed URL passes.

patches.title: Replace upstream logo images with the PrivateOffice mark
patches.rationale: About wordmark and header logo show PrivateOffice.
patches.rollback: Remove the cp loop; the original images return on rebuild.

patches.title: Materialize missing lc_pastespecial.svg icon (light + dark)
patches.rationale: Engine deterministically requests an icon upstream omits -> 404 + blank icon; copy it from the shipped paste art.

patches.title: getImageURL empty-name guard (no stray images/ request)
patches.rationale: Icon-less submenu entries build getURL('images/') -> 404; return '' for an empty imgName, matching the engine's own empty-path guard.

patches.title: Neutralize serve-time %PLACEHOLDER% branding + own (cache-busted) branding.js + remove welcome
patches.rationale: coolwsd substitutes %PRODUCT_BRANDING_NAME/URL% at serve time (the bare-word sed never sees them); set them to PrivateOffice / about:blank, empty feedback/welcome/buyproduct, drop welcome assets. branding.js: if left alone, coolwsd substitutes the %BRANDING_JS% placeholder at serve time with an UN-cache-busted <script .../branding.js>. Instead editor/Dockerfile.online itself REPLACES <!--%BRANDING_JS%--> with our own <script %SERVICE_ROOT%/browser/%VERSION%/branding.js> tag pointing at an empty inert branding.js we write, so PO-CACHE-013 appends ?b=BUILD_ID and an already-cached browser refetches it (no stale brand script). The stopgap editor/Dockerfile injects a rebranded branding.js the same way. Do NOT revert to letting coolwsd inject the tag (it would be un-cache-busted).
patches.rollback: Remove the placeholder seds (incl. the %BRANDING_JS% -> <script> replace) + the empty branding.js; keep welcome.

patches.title: Per-build ETag via ver_suffix = BUILD_ID (coolwsd.xml)
patches.rationale: ETag derives from the compile-time hash, which our content patches never change; stamp BUILD_ID into ver_suffix so each patched rebuild yields a fresh ETag.
patches.rollback: Remove the sed; the ETag reverts to the upstream hash.

patches.title: Cache-bust cool.html-referenced assets with ?b=BUILD_ID
patches.rationale: Browsers holding a 128-day max-age copy won't revalidate; ?b= forces a refetch of the patched bundle.css/js + l10n catalogs + the branding.js tag (injected by PO-BRAND-011) on next open.

patches.title: Source-patch mechanism: apply editor/patches/*.patch to the cloned Online source
patches.rationale: Channel real source-level changes to coolwsd / the browser bundle through reviewable unified diffs applied with `git apply` right after `git clone` and before `configure` - not post-build sed on built artifacts, not runtime overrides. No-op when no .patch is present; a failed apply breaks the build on purpose. Pinned to online_from_source.ref; rebase on re-pin. See editor/patches/README.md.
patches.rollback: Remove the `COPY patches` + `git apply` step from editor/Dockerfile.online and the editor/patches/ dir.
patches.status: scaffold: mechanism wired + self-tested at build; no production source patches yet (README only).

patches.title: Snapshot the unmerged source-built client catalog to /usr/share/coolwsd/upstream-l10n
patches.rationale: Before the override merge, copy the freshly-built ui-<lang>.json to a non-served path that ships in the image. This is the TRUE upstream baseline for the override overlap check (scripts/check-l10n-overrides.py --upstream-from-active): the exact catalog the merge writes into, read from the SAME running container as the active catalog - not the base image's prebuilt browser dist (a different build that can drift).
patches.rollback: Remove the cp-to-upstream-l10n RUN step; the offline base-image approximation (make l10n-overlap-offline) still works.

patches.title: Localize notebookbar function-name dropdowns for all locales (fixes insert + display in RU etc.)
patches.render_path: n/a (build-time source patch + parse-blocking head script po-funcnames.js)
patches.rationale: Upstream getLocalizedFuncName localizes only de/fr/es, so the Formulas-tab function dropdowns (Date & Time, Financial, Logical, Math & Trig, …) emit the ENGLISH function name both as the visible label and inside .uno:InsertFunction?FunctionName=. A localized LO core only accepts the LOCALIZED name (RU needs СЕГОДНЯ, not TODAY), so in RU and every non-en/de/fr/es locale the dropdown shows English AND fails to insert. Fix: patch getLocalizedFuncName to consult window.PO_FN_NAMES - a per-locale {english:localized} map extracted from the core for.mo (RID_STRLIST_FUNCTION_NAMES) + sca.mo (*_FUNCNAME_*) by scripts/build-l10n-funcnames.py into editor/po-funcnames.js, delivered as a parse-blocking <head> script (like po-toggle.js) that runs before the deferred bundle.js. Covers all localizable functions across 12 locales. The same patch also removes the 9 dead Excel-import compatibility aliases (…_ADD / …_EXCEL2003, e.g. CUMIPMT_ADD, WEEKNUM_EXCEL2003) from the dropdowns: they are not real insertable functions (absent from the core name table) and did nothing on click; their real equivalents (CUMIPMT, WEEKNUM, …) remain. Confirmed an upstream defect on the clean base image.
patches.rollback: Remove editor/patches/0001-*.patch, the po-funcnames.js COPY + cool.html inject in editor/Dockerfile.online, and editor/po-funcnames.js. Regenerate the map (scripts/build-l10n-funcnames.py) on every engine re-pin.

patches.title: TreeView: treat row -1 as clear-selection, not an error (no console warn)
patches.rationale: The core sends jsdialogaction select position=-1 (clear selection) as the user clicks around the Calc Function Wizard tree; selectEntryByRow(-1) misses the row map and logs console.warn('TreeView onSelect: row -1 not found') on every click. Treat row<0 as a normal deselect (unselect all, return) without warning. Confirmed upstream on the clean base image; benign but noisy.

patches.title: Notebookbar: keep the Formulas tab active after inserting a function (fix name typo)
patches.rationale: updateTabsVisibilityForContext has a guard meant to keep the Formulas tab selected on a Calc context change (so inserting a function from the Formulas tab doesn't jump the ribbon to Home). But it checks isTabSelected('Formulas') while the Calc tab's name is 'Formula' (Control.NotebookbarCalc.js) — so the guard never fires and the ribbon switches to Home on every function insert. Fix the name to 'Formula'. Upstream typo; became visible once RU function insertion worked.
